error.signature: Error: Error acquiring the state lock: ConditionalCheckFailedException

verdict.summary: Terraform S3 backend uses DynamoDB for state locking. If a previous terraform apply was killed (Ctrl+C, CI timeout, OOM), the lock remains forever. force-unlock is scary but sometimes necessary.

dead_ends.action: Wait for the lock to expire automatically
dead_ends.why_fails: DynamoDB locks have no TTL by default. The lock will remain forever until manually removed.

dead_ends.action: Run another terraform apply hoping it will override the lock
dead_ends.why_fails: Concurrent applies with locked state cause state corruption. Never run parallel applies.

dead_ends.action: Delete the DynamoDB lock item directly from console
dead_ends.why_fails: Deleting the item works but bypasses Terraform's lock check. If another process IS running, state corruption follows.

workarounds.action: Use terraform force-unlock with the lock ID
workarounds.how: terraform force-unlock LOCK_ID  # Lock ID is shown in the error message

workarounds.action: Check if another process is actually running before force-unlock
workarounds.how: Check CI/CD pipelines, other terminals. Only force-unlock if you're SURE no other apply is running.

workarounds.action: Add -lock-timeout=5m to terraform commands in CI to handle transient locks
workarounds.how: terraform apply -lock-timeout=300s  # wait up to 5 minutes for lock release
